NKJV.1: Now when He concluded all His sayings in the hearing of the people, He entered Capernaum.
NKJV.2: And a certain centurion’s servant, who was dear to him, was sick and ready to die.
NKJV.3: So when he heard about Jesus, he sent elders of the Jews to Him, pleading with Him to come and heal his servant.
NKJV.4: And when they came to Jesus, they begged Him earnestly, saying that the one for whom He should do this was deserving,
NKJV.5: “for he loves our nation, and has built us a synagogue.”
NKJV.6: Then Jesus went with them. And when He was already not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to Him, saying to Him, “Lord, do not trouble Yourself, for I am not worthy that You should enter under my roof.
NKJV.7: Therefore I did not even think myself worthy to come to You. But say the word, and my servant will be healed.
NKJV.8: For I also am a man placed under authority, having soldiers under me. And I say to one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and to another, ‘Come,’ and he comes; and to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”
NKJV.9: When Jesus heard these things, He marveled at him, and turned around and said to the crowd that followed Him, “I say to you, I have not found such great faith, not even in Israel!”
NKJV.10: And those who were sent, returning to the house, found the servant well who had been sick.
NKJV.11: Now it happened, the day after, that He went into a city called Nain; and many of His disciples went with Him, and a large crowd.
NKJV.12: And when He came near the gate of the city, behold, a dead man was being carried out, the only son of his mother; and she was a widow. And a large crowd from the city was with her.
NKJV.13: When the Lord saw her, He had compassion on her and said to her, “Do not weep.”
NKJV.14: Then He came and touched the open coffin, and those who carried him stood still. And He said, “Young man, I say to you, arise.”
NKJV.15: So he who was dead sat up and began to speak. And He presented him to his mother.
NKJV.16: Then fear came upon all, and they glorified God, saying, “A great prophet has risen up among us”; and, “God has visited His people.”
NKJV.17: And this report about Him went throughout all Judea and all the surrounding region.
NKJV.18: Then the disciples of John reported to him concerning all these things.
NKJV.19: And John, calling two of his disciples to him, sent them to Jesus, saying, “Are You the Coming One, or do we look for another?”
NKJV.20: When the men had come to Him, they said, “John the Baptist has sent us to You, saying, ‘Are You the Coming One, or do we look for another?’ ”
NKJV.21: And that very hour He cured many of infirmities, afflictions, and evil spirits; and to many blind He gave sight.
NKJV.22: Jesus answered and said to them, “Go and tell John the things you have seen and heard: that the blind see, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, the poor have the gospel preached to them.
NKJV.23: And blessed is he who is not offended because of Me.”
NKJV.24: When the messengers of John had departed, He began to speak to the multitudes concerning John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?
NKJV.25: But what did you go out to see? A man clothed in soft garments? Indeed those who are gorgeously appareled and live in luxury are in kings’ courts.
NKJV.26: But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I say to you, and more than a prophet.
NKJV.27: This is he of whom it is written: ‘Behold, I send My messenger before Your face, Who will prepare Your way before You.’
NKJV.28: For I say to you, among those born of women there is not a  greater prophet than John the Baptist; but he who is least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.”
NKJV.29: And when all the people heard Him, even the tax collectors justified God, having been baptized with the baptism of John.
NKJV.30: But the Pharisees and lawyers rejected the will of God for themselves, not having been baptized by him.
NKJV.31: And the Lord said, “To what then shall I liken the men of this generation, and what are they like?
NKJV.32: They are like children sitting in the marketplace and calling to one another, saying: ‘We played the flute for you, And you did not dance; We mourned to you, And you did not weep.’
NKJV.33: For  John the Baptist came  neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, ‘He has a demon.’
NKJV.34: The Son of Man has come  eating and drinking, and you say, ‘Look, a glutton and a winebibber, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’
NKJV.35: But wisdom is justified by all her children.”
NKJV.36: Then one of the Pharisees asked Him to eat with him. And He went to the Pharisee’s house, and sat down to eat.
NKJV.37: And behold, a woman in the city who was a sinner, when she knew that Jesus sat at the table in the Pharisee’s house, brought an alabaster flask of fragrant oil,
NKJV.38: and stood at His feet behind Him weeping; and she began to wash His feet with her tears, and wiped them with the hair of her head; and she kissed His feet and anointed them with the fragrant oil.
NKJV.39: Now when the Pharisee who had invited Him saw this, he spoke to himself, saying, “This Man, if He were a prophet, would know who and what manner of woman this is who is touching Him, for she is a sinner.”
NKJV.40: And Jesus answered and said to him, “Simon, I have something to say to you.” So he said, “Teacher, say it.”
NKJV.41: “There was a certain creditor who had two debtors. One owed five hundred  denarii, and the other fifty.
NKJV.42: And when they had nothing with which to repay, he freely forgave them both. Tell Me, therefore, which of them will love him more?”
NKJV.43: Simon answered and said, “I suppose the one whom he forgave more.” And He said to him, “You have rightly judged.”
NKJV.44: Then He turned to the woman and said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? I entered your house; you gave Me no water for My feet, but she has washed My feet with her tears and wiped them with the hair of her head.
NKJV.45: You gave Me no  kiss, but this woman has not ceased to kiss My feet since the time I came in.
NKJV.46: You did not anoint My head with oil, but this woman has anointed My feet with fragrant oil.
NKJV.47: Therefore I say to you, her sins, which are many, are forgiven, for she loved much. But to whom little is forgiven, the same loves little.”
NKJV.48: Then He said to her, “Your sins are forgiven.”
NKJV.49: And those who sat at the table with Him began to say to themselves, “Who is this who even forgives sins?”
NKJV.50: Then He said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.”

ESV.1: After he had finished all his sayings in the hearing of the people, he entered Capernaum.
ESV.2: Now a centurion had a servant  who was sick and at the point of death, who was highly valued by him.
ESV.3: When the centurion  heard about Jesus, he sent to him elders of the Jews, asking him to come and heal his servant.
ESV.4: And when they came to Jesus, they pleaded with him earnestly, saying, “He is worthy to have you do this for him,
ESV.5: for he loves our nation, and he is the one who built us our synagogue.”
ESV.6: And Jesus went with them. When he was not far from the house, the centurion sent friends, saying to him, “Lord, do not trouble yourself, for I am not worthy to have you come under my roof.
ESV.7: Therefore I did not presume to come to you. But say the word, and let my servant be healed.
ESV.8: For I too am a man set under authority, with soldiers under me: and I say to one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and to another, ‘Come,’ and he comes; and to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”
ESV.9: When Jesus heard these things, he marveled at him, and turning to the crowd that followed him, said, “I tell you, not even in Israel have I found such faith.”
ESV.10: And when those who had been sent returned to the house, they found the servant well.
ESV.11: Soon afterward  he went to a town called Nain, and his disciples and a great crowd went with him.
ESV.12: As he drew near to the gate of the town, behold, a man who had died was being carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow, and a considerable crowd from the town was with her.
ESV.13: And when the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her and said to her, “Do not weep.”
ESV.14: Then he came up and touched the bier, and the bearers stood still. And he said, “Young man, I say to you, arise.”
ESV.15: And the dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus  gave him to his mother.
ESV.16: Fear seized them all, and they glorified God, saying, “A great prophet has arisen among us!” and “God has visited his people!”
ESV.17: And this report about him spread through the whole of Judea and all the surrounding country.
ESV.18: The disciples of John reported all these things to him. And John,
ESV.19: calling two of his disciples to him, sent them to the Lord, saying, “Are you the one who is to come, or shall we look for another?”
ESV.20: And when the men had come to him, they said, “John the Baptist has sent us to you, saying, ‘Are you the one who is to come, or shall we look for another?’”
ESV.21: In that hour he healed many people of diseases and plagues and evil spirits, and on many who were blind he bestowed sight.
ESV.22: And he answered them, “Go and tell John what you have seen and heard: the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, lepers  are cleansed, and the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, the poor have good news preached to them.
ESV.23: And blessed is the one who is not offended by me.”
ESV.24: When John's messengers had gone, Jesus  began to speak to the crowds concerning John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?
ESV.25: What then did you go out to see? A man dressed in soft clothing? Behold, those who are dressed in splendid clothing and live in luxury are in kings' courts.
ESV.26: What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
ESV.27: This is he of whom it is written,  “‘Behold, I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way before you.’
ESV.28: I tell you, among those born of women none is greater than John. Yet the one who is least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.”
ESV.29: (When all the people heard this, and the tax collectors too, they declared God just,  having been baptized with the baptism of John,
ESV.30: but the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected the purpose of God for themselves, not having been baptized by him.)
ESV.31: “To what then shall I compare the people of this generation, and what are they like?
ESV.32: They are like children sitting in the marketplace and calling to one another, “‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not weep.’
ESV.33: For John the Baptist has come eating no bread and drinking no wine, and you say, ‘He has a demon.’
ESV.34: The Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, ‘Look at him! A glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’
ESV.35: Yet wisdom is justified by all her children.”
ESV.36: One of the Pharisees asked him to eat with him, and he went into the Pharisee's house and reclined at table.
ESV.37: And behold, a woman of the city, who was a sinner, when she learned that he was reclining at table in the Pharisee's house, brought an alabaster flask of ointment,
ESV.38: and standing behind him at his feet, weeping, she began to wet his feet with her tears and wiped them with the hair of her head and kissed his feet and anointed them with the ointment.
ESV.39: Now when the Pharisee who had invited him saw this, he said to himself, “If this man were a prophet, he would have known who and what sort of woman this is who is touching him, for she is a sinner.”
ESV.40: And Jesus answering said to him, “Simon, I have something to say to you.” And he answered, “Say it, Teacher.”
ESV.41: “A certain moneylender had two debtors. One owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.
ESV.42: When they could not pay, he cancelled the debt of both. Now which of them will love him more?”
ESV.43: Simon answered, “The one, I suppose, for whom he cancelled the larger debt.” And he said to him, “You have judged rightly.”
ESV.44: Then turning toward the woman he said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? I entered your house; you gave me no water for my feet, but she has wet my feet with her tears and wiped them with her hair.
ESV.45: You gave me no kiss, but from the time I came in she has not ceased to kiss my feet.
ESV.46: You did not anoint my head with oil, but she has anointed my feet with ointment.
ESV.47: Therefore I tell you, her sins, which are many, are forgiven — for she loved much. But he who is forgiven little, loves little.”
ESV.48: And he said to her,  “Your sins are forgiven.”
ESV.49: Then those who were at table with him began to say among  themselves, “Who is this, who even forgives sins?”
ESV.50: And he said to the woman,  “Your faith has saved you; go in peace.”

NASB.1: When He had completed all His discourse in the hearing of the people, He went to Capernaum.
NASB.2: And a centurion's slave, who was highly regarded by him, was sick and about to die.
NASB.3: When he heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders asking Him to come and save the life of his slave.
NASB.4: When they came to Jesus, they earnestly implored Him, saying, 'He is worthy for You to grant this to him;
NASB.5: for he loves our nation and it was he who built us our synagogue.'
NASB.6: Now Jesus [started] on His way with them; and when He was not far from the house, the centurion sent friends, saying to Him, 'Lord, do not trouble Yourself further, for I am not worthy for You to come under my roof;
NASB.7: for this reason I did not even consider myself worthy to come to You, but [just] say the word, and my servant will be healed.
NASB.8: 'For I also am a man placed under authority, with soldiers under me; and I say to this one, 'Go!' and he goes, and to another, 'Come!' and he comes, and to my slave, 'Do this!' and he does it.'
NASB.9: Now when Jesus heard this, He marveled at him, and turned and said to the crowd that was following Him, 'I say to you, not even in Israel have I found such great faith.'
NASB.10: When those who had been sent returned to the house, they found the slave in good health.
NASB.11: Soon afterwards He went to a city called Nain; and His disciples were going along with Him, accompanied by a large crowd.
NASB.12: Now as He approached the gate of the city, a dead man was being carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow; and a sizeable crowd from the city was with her.
NASB.13: When the Lord saw her, He felt compassion for her, and said to her, 'Do not weep.'
NASB.14: And He came up and touched the coffin; and the bearers came to a halt. And He said, 'Young man, I say to you, arise!'
NASB.15: The dead man sat up and began to speak. And [Jesus] gave him back to his mother.
NASB.16: Fear gripped them all, and they [began] glorifying God, saying, 'A great prophet has arisen among us!' and, 'God has visited His people!'
NASB.17: This report concerning Him went out all over Judea and in all the surrounding district.
NASB.18: The disciples of John reported to him about all these things.
NASB.19: Summoning two of his disciples, John sent them to the Lord, saying, 'Are You the Expected One, or do we look for someone else?'
NASB.20: When the men came to Him, they said, 'John the Baptist has sent us to You, to ask, 'Are You the Expected One, or do we look for someone else?''
NASB.21: At that very time He cured many [people] of diseases and afflictions and evil spirits; and He gave sight to many [who were] blind.
NASB.22: And He answered and said to them, 'Go and report to John what you have seen and heard: [the] BLIND RECEIVE SIGHT, [the] lame walk, [the] lepers are cleansed, and [the] deaf hear, [the] dead are raised up, [the] POOR HAVE THE GOSPEL PREACHED TO THEM.
NASB.23: 'Blessed is he who does not take offense at Me.'
NASB.24: When the messengers of John had left, He began to speak to the crowds about John, 'What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?
NASB.25: 'But what did you go out to see? A man dressed in soft clothing? Those who are splendidly clothed and live in luxury are [found] in royal palaces!
NASB.26: 'But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I say to you, and one who is more than a prophet.
NASB.27: 'This is the one about whom it is written, 'BEHOLD, I SEND MY MESSENGER AHEAD OF YOU, WHO WILL PREPARE YOUR WAY BEFORE YOU.'
NASB.28: 'I say to you, among those born of women there is no one greater than John; yet he who is least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.'
NASB.29: When all the people and the tax collectors heard [this], they acknowledged God's justice, having been baptized with the baptism of John.
NASB.30: But the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected God's purpose for themselves, not having been baptized by John.
NASB.31: 'To what then shall I compare the men of this generation, and what are they like?
NASB.32: 'They are like children who sit in the market place and call to one another, and they say, 'We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not weep.'
NASB.33: 'For John the Baptist has come eating no bread and drinking no wine, and you say, 'He has a demon!'
NASB.34: 'The Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, 'Behold, a gluttonous man and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!'
NASB.35: 'Yet wisdom is vindicated by all her children.'
NASB.36: Now one of the Pharisees was requesting Him to dine with him, and He entered the Pharisee's house and reclined [at the table].
NASB.37: And there was a woman in the city who was a sinner; and when she learned that He was reclining [at the table] in the Pharisee's house, she brought an alabaster vial of perfume,
NASB.38: and standing behind [Him] at His feet, weeping, she began to wet His feet with her tears, and kept wiping them with the hair of her head, and kissing His feet and anointing them with the perfume.
NASB.39: Now when the Pharisee who had invited Him saw this, he said to himself, 'If this man were a prophet He would know who and what sort of person this woman is who is touching Him, that she is a sinner.'
NASB.40: And Jesus answered him, 'Simon, I have something to say to you.' And he replied, 'Say it, Teacher.'
NASB.41: 'A moneylender had two debtors: one owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.
NASB.42: 'When they were unable to repay, he graciously forgave them both. So which of them will love him more?'
NASB.43: Simon answered and said, 'I suppose the one whom he forgave more.' And He said to him, 'You have judged correctly.'
NASB.44: Turning toward the woman, He said to Simon, 'Do you see this woman? I entered your house; you gave Me no water for My feet, but she has wet My feet with her tears and wiped them with her hair.
NASB.45: 'You gave Me no kiss; but she, since the time I came in, has not ceased to kiss My feet.
NASB.46: 'You did not anoint My head with oil, but she anointed My feet with perfume.

NLT.1: When Jesus had finished saying all this to the people, he returned to Capernaum.
NLT.2: At that time the highly valued slave of a Roman officer was sick and near death.
NLT.3: When the officer heard about Jesus, he sent some respected Jewish elders to ask him to come and heal his slave.
NLT.4: So they earnestly begged Jesus to help the man. “If anyone deserves your help, he does,” they said,
NLT.5: “for he loves the Jewish people and even built a synagogue for us.”
NLT.6: So Jesus went with them. But just before they arrived at the house, the officer sent some friends to say, “Lord, don’t trouble yourself by coming to my home, for I am not worthy of such an honor.
NLT.7: I am not even worthy to come and meet you. Just say the word from where you are, and my servant will be healed.
NLT.8: I know this because I am under the authority of my superior officers, and I have authority over my soldiers. I only need to say, ‘Go,’ and they go, or ‘Come,’ and they come. And if I say to my slaves, ‘Do this,’ they do it.”
NLT.9: When Jesus heard this, he was amazed. Turning to the crowd that was following him, he said, “I tell you, I haven’t seen faith like this in all Israel!”
NLT.10: And when the officer’s friends returned to his house, they found the slave completely healed.
NLT.11: Soon afterward Jesus went with his disciples to the village of Nain, and a large crowd followed him.
NLT.12: A funeral procession was coming out as he approached the village gate. The young man who had died was a widow’s only son, and a large crowd from the village was with her.
NLT.13: When the Lord saw her, his heart overflowed with compassion. “Don’t cry!” he said.
NLT.14: Then he walked over to the coffin and touched it, and the bearers stopped. “Young man,” he said, “I tell you, get up.”
NLT.15: Then the dead boy sat up and began to talk! And Jesus gave him back to his mother.
NLT.16: Great fear swept the crowd, and they praised God, saying, “A mighty prophet has risen among us,” and “God has visited his people today.”
NLT.17: And the news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding countryside.
NLT.18: The disciples of John the Baptist told John about everything Jesus was doing. So John called for two of his disciples,
NLT.19: and he sent them to the Lord to ask him, “Are you the Messiah we’ve been expecting, or should we keep looking for someone else?”
NLT.20: John’s two disciples found Jesus and said to him, “John the Baptist sent us to ask, ‘Are you the Messiah we’ve been expecting, or should we keep looking for someone else?’”
NLT.21: At that very time, Jesus cured many people of their diseases, illnesses, and evil spirits, and he restored sight to many who were blind.
NLT.22: Then he told John’s disciples, “Go back to John and tell him what you have seen and heard — the blind see, the lame walk, those with leprosy are cured, the deaf hear, the dead are raised to life, and the Good News is being preached to the poor.”
NLT.23: And he added, “God blesses those who do not fall away because of me. ”
NLT.24: After John’s disciples left, Jesus began talking about him to the crowds. “What kind of man did you go into the wilderness to see? Was he a weak reed, swayed by every breath of wind?
NLT.25: Or were you expecting to see a man dressed in expensive clothes? No, people who wear beautiful clothes and live in luxury are found in palaces.
NLT.26: Were you looking for a prophet? Yes, and he is more than a prophet.
NLT.27:
John is the man to whom the Scriptures refer when they say, ‘Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you,
and he will prepare your way before you.’
NLT.28: I tell you, of all who have ever lived, none is greater than John. Yet even the least person in the Kingdom of God is greater than he is!”
NLT.29: When they heard this, all the people — even the tax collectors — agreed that God’s way was right, for they had been baptized by John.
NLT.30: But the Pharisees and experts in religious law rejected God’s plan for them, for they had refused John’s baptism.
NLT.31: “To what can I compare the people of this generation?” Jesus asked. “How can I describe them?
NLT.32:
They are like children playing a game in the public square. They complain to their friends, ‘We played wedding songs,
and you didn’t dance,
so we played funeral songs,
and you didn’t weep.’
NLT.33: For John the Baptist didn’t spend his time eating bread or drinking wine, and you say, ‘He’s possessed by a demon.’
NLT.34: The Son of Man, on the other hand, feasts and drinks, and you say, ‘He’s a glutton and a drunkard, and a friend of tax collectors and other sinners!’
NLT.35: But wisdom is shown to be right by the lives of those who follow it. ”
NLT.36: One of the Pharisees asked Jesus to have dinner with him, so Jesus went to his home and sat down to eat.
NLT.37: When a certain immoral woman from that city heard he was eating there, she brought a beautiful alabaster jar filled with expensive perfume.
NLT.38: Then she knelt behind him at his feet, weeping. Her tears fell on his feet, and she wiped them off with her hair. Then she kept kissing his feet and putting perfume on them.
NLT.39: When the Pharisee who had invited him saw this, he said to himself, “If this man were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him. She’s a sinner!”
NLT.40: Then Jesus answered his thoughts. “Simon,” he said to the Pharisee, “I have something to say to you.” “Go ahead, Teacher,” Simon replied.
NLT.41: Then Jesus told him this story: “A man loaned money to two people — 500 pieces of silver to one and 50 pieces to the other.
NLT.42: But neither of them could repay him, so he kindly forgave them both, canceling their debts. Who do you suppose loved him more after that?”
NLT.43: Simon answered, “I suppose the one for whom he canceled the larger debt.” “That’s right,” Jesus said.
NLT.44: Then he turned to the woman and said to Simon, “Look at this woman kneeling here. When I entered your home, you didn’t offer me water to wash the dust from my feet, but she has washed them with her tears and wiped them with her hair.
NLT.45: You didn’t greet me with a kiss, but from the time I first came in, she has not stopped kissing my feet.
NLT.46: You neglected the courtesy of olive oil to anoint my head, but she has anointed my feet with rare perfume.
NLT.47: “I tell you, her sins — and they are many — have been forgiven, so she has shown me much love. But a person who is forgiven little shows only little love.”
NLT.48: Then Jesus said to the woman, “Your sins are forgiven.”
NLT.49: The men at the table said among themselves, “Who is this man, that he goes around forgiving sins?”
NLT.50: And Jesus said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you; go in peace.”

WEB.2: A certain centurion’s servant, who was dear to him, was sick and at the point of death.
WEB.3: When he heard about Jesus, he sent to him elders of the Jews, asking him to come and save his servant.
WEB.4: When they came to Jesus, they begged him earnestly, saying, “He is worthy for you to do this for him,
WEB.5: for he loves our nation, and he built our synagogue for us.”
WEB.6: Jesus went with them. When he was now not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to him, saying to him, “Lord, don’t trouble yourself, for I am not worthy for you to come under my roof.
WEB.7: Therefore I didn’t even think myself worthy to come to you; but say the word, and my servant will be healed.
WEB.8: For I also am a man placed under authority, having under myself soldiers. I tell this one, ‘Go!’ and he goes; and to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes; and to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”
WEB.9: When Jesus heard these things, he marveled at him, and turned and said to the multitude who followed him, “I tell you, I have not found such great faith, no, not in Israel.”
WEB.10: Those who were sent, returning to the house, found that the servant who had been sick was well.
WEB.11: Soon afterwards, he went to a city called Nain. Many of his disciples, along with a great multitude, went with him.
WEB.12: Now when he came near to the gate of the city, behold, one who was dead was carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow. Many people of the city were with her.
WEB.13: When the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her, and said to her, “Don’t cry.”
WEB.14: He came near and touched the coffin, and the bearers stood still. He said, “Young man, I tell you, arise!”
WEB.15: He who was dead sat up, and began to speak. And he gave him to his mother.
WEB.16: Fear took hold of all, and they glorified God, saying, “A great prophet has arisen among us!” and, “God has visited his people!”
WEB.17: This report went out concerning him in the whole of Judea, and in all the surrounding region.
WEB.18: The disciples of John told him about all these things.
WEB.19: John, calling to himself two of his disciples, sent them to Jesus, saying, “Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for another?”
WEB.20: When the men had come to him, they said, “John the Baptizer has sent us to you, saying, ‘Are you he who comes, or should we look for another?’”
WEB.21: In that hour he cured many of diseases and plagues and evil spirits; and to many who were blind he gave sight.
WEB.22: Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John the things which you have seen and heard: that the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have good news preached to them.
WEB.23: Blessed is he who finds no occasion for stumbling in me.”
WEB.24: When John’s messengers had departed, he began to tell the multitudes about John, “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?
WEB.25: But what did you go out to see? A man clothed in soft clothing? Behold, those who are gorgeously dressed, and live delicately, are in kings’ courts.
WEB.26: But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and much more than a prophet.
WEB.27: This is he of whom it is written,  ‘Behold, I send my messenger before your face,  who will prepare your way before you.’
WEB.28: “For I tell you, among those who are born of women there is not a greater prophet than John the Baptizer, yet he who is least in God’s Kingdom is greater than he.”
WEB.29: When all the people and the tax collectors heard this, they declared God to be just, having been baptized with John’s baptism.
WEB.30: But the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected the counsel of God, not being baptized by him themselves.
WEB.31: “To what then will I liken the people of this generation? What are they like?
WEB.32: They are like children who sit in the marketplace, and call to one another, saying, ‘We piped to you, and you didn’t dance. We mourned, and you didn’t weep.’
WEB.33: For John the Baptizer came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, ‘He has a demon.’
WEB.34: The Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, ‘Behold, a gluttonous man, and a drunkard; a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’
WEB.35: Wisdom is justified by all her children.”
WEB.36: One of the Pharisees invited him to eat with him. He entered into the Pharisee’s house, and sat at the table.
WEB.37: Behold, a woman in the city who was a sinner, when she knew that he was reclining in the Pharisee’s house, she brought an alabaster jar of ointment.
WEB.38: Standing behind at his feet weeping, she began to wet his feet with her tears, and she wiped them with the hair of her head, kissed his feet, and anointed them with the ointment.
WEB.39: Now when the Pharisee who had invited him saw it, he said to himself, “This man, if he were a prophet, would have perceived who and what kind of woman this is who touches him, that she is a sinner.”
WEB.40: Jesus answered him, “Simon, I have something to tell you.”  He said, “Teacher, say on.”
WEB.41: “A certain lender had two debtors. The one owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.
WEB.42: When they couldn’t pay, he forgave them both. Which of them therefore will love him most?”
WEB.43: Simon answered, “He, I suppose, to whom he forgave the most.” He said to him, “You have judged correctly.”
WEB.44: Turning to the woman, he said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? I entered into your house, and you gave me no water for my feet, but she has wet my feet with her tears, and wiped them with the hair of her head.
WEB.45: You gave me no kiss, but she, since the time I came in, has not ceased to kiss my feet.
WEB.46: You didn’t anoint my head with oil, but she has anointed my feet with ointment.
WEB.47: Therefore I tell you, her sins, which are many, are forgiven, for she loved much. But to whom little is forgiven, the same loves little.”
WEB.48: He said to her, “Your sins are forgiven.”
WEB.49: Those who sat at the table with him began to say to themselves, “Who is this who even forgives sins?”
WEB.50: He said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.”

AMP.1: After He had finished all that He had to say in the hearing of the people, He went to Capernaum.
AMP.2: Now a [Roman] centurion's slave, who was highly regarded by him, was sick and on the verge of death.
AMP.3: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to Him, asking Him to come and save the life of his slave.
AMP.4: When they reached Jesus, they pleaded with Him earnestly [to come], saying, "He is worthy for You to do this for him,
AMP.5: because he loves our nation and he built us our synagogue [at his own expense]."
AMP.6: And Jesus went with them. But when He was near the house, the centurion sent friends to Him, saying, "Lord, do not trouble Yourself further, for I am not worthy for You to come under my roof.
AMP.7: Therefore I did not even consider myself worthy to come to You. But just speak a word, and my slave will be healed.
AMP.8: For I also am a man subject to authority, with soldiers under me; and I say to this one, 'Go,' and he goes, and to another, 'Come,' and he comes, and to my slave, 'Do this,' and he does it."
AMP.9: Now when Jesus heard this, He was amazed at him, and turned and said to the crowd that was following Him, "I say to you, not even in Israel have I found such great faith [as this man's]."
AMP.10: When the messengers who had been sent returned to the house, they found the slave in good health.
AMP.11: Soon afterward Jesus went to a city called Nain [near Nazareth], and His disciples and a large crowd accompanied Him.
AMP.12: Now as He approached the city gate, a dead man was being carried out—the only son of his mother, and she was a widow. And a large crowd from the city was with her [in the funeral procession].
AMP.13: When the Lord saw her, He felt [great] compassion for her, and said to her, "Do not weep."
AMP.14: And He came up and touched the bier [on which the body rested], and the pallbearers stood still. And He said, "Young man, I say to you, arise [from death]!"
AMP.15: The man who was dead sat up and began to speak. And Jesus gave him back to his mother.
AMP.16: Fear and profound awe gripped them all, and they began glorifying and honoring and praising God, saying, "A great prophet has arisen among us!" and, "God has visited His people [to help and care for and provide for them]!"
AMP.17: This news about Him spread through all of Judea and in all the surrounding countryside.
AMP.18: John's disciples brought word to him [in prison] of all these things.
AMP.19: John called two of his disciples and sent them to the Lord, saying, "Are You the Expected One (the Messiah), or should we look for someone else?"
AMP.20: So the men came to Jesus and said, "John the Baptist sent us to You to ask, 'Are You the Expected One (the Messiah), or should we look for someone else?'"
AMP.21: At that very hour Jesus healed many people of sicknesses and infirmities and evil spirits; and He gave [the gracious gift of] sight to many who were blind.
AMP.22: So He replied to them, "Go and tell John about everything you have seen and heard: the <e>blind receive sight</e>, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the <e>poor have the good news</e> (gospel) <e>preached to them</e>.
AMP.23: Blessed [joyful, spiritually favored] is he who does not take offense at Me."
AMP.24: When John's messengers left, Jesus began speaking to the crowds about John: "What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?
AMP.25: But what did you go out to see? A man dressed in soft clothing [entirely unsuited for the harsh wilderness]? Those who wear splendid clothing and live in luxury are in royal palaces!
AMP.26: But what did you [really] go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I say to you, and one far more [eminent and remarkable] than a prophet [who foretells the future].
AMP.27:
This is the one of whom it is written [by the prophet Malachi],
'Behold, I send My messenger ahead of You,
Who will prepare Your way before You.'
AMP.28: I tell you, among those born of women there is no one greater than John; yet he who is least in the kingdom of God is greater [in privilege] than he."
AMP.29: All the people and the tax collectors who heard Jesus, acknowledged [the validity of] God's justice, having been baptized with the baptism of John.
AMP.30: But the Pharisees and the lawyers [who were experts in the Mosaic Law] annulled and set aside God's purpose for themselves, not having been baptized by John.
AMP.31: "To what then shall I compare the people of this generation [who set aside God's plan], and what are they like?
AMP.32: They are like children sitting in the market place and calling to one another, and saying, 'We played the flute for you [pretending to be at a wedding], and you did not dance; we sang a dirge [pretending to be at a funeral], and you did not weep [so nothing we did appealed to you].'
AMP.33: For John the Baptist has come neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, 'He has a demon!'
AMP.34: The Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, 'Look, a man who is a glutton and a [heavy] wine-drinker, a friend of tax collectors and sinners [including non-observant Jews].'
AMP.35: Yet wisdom is vindicated and shown to be right by all her children [by the lifestyle, moral character, and good deeds of her followers]."
AMP.36: One of the Pharisees asked Jesus to eat with him, and He went into the Pharisee's house [in the region of Galilee] and reclined at the table.
AMP.37: Now there was a woman in the city who was [known as] a sinner; and when she found out that He was reclining at the table in the Pharisee's house, she brought an alabaster vial of perfume;
AMP.38: and standing behind Him at His feet, weeping, she began wetting His feet with her tears, and wiped them with the hair of her head, and [respectfully] kissed His feet [as an act signifying both affection and submission] and anointed them with the perfume.
AMP.39: Now when [Simon] the Pharisee who had invited Him saw this, he said to himself, "If this Man were a prophet He would know who and what sort of woman this is who is touching Him, that she is a [notorious] sinner [an outcast, devoted to sin]."
AMP.40: Jesus, answering, said to the Pharisee, "Simon, I have something to say to you." And he replied, "Teacher, say it."
AMP.41: "A certain moneylender had two debtors: one owed him five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.
AMP.42: When they had no means of repaying [the debts], he freely forgave them both. So which of them will love him more?"
AMP.43: Simon answered, "The one, I take it, for whom he forgave more." Jesus said to him, "You have decided correctly."
AMP.44: Then turning toward the woman, He said to Simon, "Do you see this woman? I came into your house [but you failed to extend to Me the usual courtesies shown to a guest]; you gave Me no water for My feet, but she has wet My feet with her tears and wiped them with her hair [demonstrating her love].
AMP.45: You gave Me no [welcoming] kiss, but from the moment I came in, she has not ceased to kiss My feet.
AMP.46: You did not [even] anoint My head with [ordinary] oil, but she has anointed My feet with [costly and rare] perfume.
AMP.47: Therefore I say to you, her sins, which are many, are forgiven, for she loved much; but he who is forgiven little, loves little."
AMP.48: Then He said to her, "Your sins are forgiven."
AMP.49: Those who were reclining at the table with Him began saying among themselves, "Who is this who even forgives sins?"
AMP.50: Jesus said to the woman, "Your faith [in Me] has saved you; go in peace [free from the distress experienced because of sin]."

MSG.1: When he finished speaking to the people, he entered Capernaum.
MSG.2: A Roman captain there had a servant who was on his deathbed. He prized him highly and didn't want to lose him.
MSG.3: When he heard Jesus was back, he sent leaders from the Jewish community asking him to come and heal his servant.
MSG.4: They came to Jesus and urged him to do it, saying, "He deserves this.
MSG.5: He loves our people. He even built our meeting place."
MSG.6: Jesus went with them. When he was still quite far from the house, the captain sent friends to tell him, "Master, you don't have to go to all this trouble. I'm not that good a person, you know. I'd be embarrassed for you to come to my house,
MSG.7: even embarrassed to come to you in person. Just give the order and my servant will get well.
MSG.8: I'm a man under orders; I also give orders. I tell one soldier, 'Go,' and he goes; another, 'Come,' and he comes; my slave, 'Do this,' and he does it."
MSG.9: Taken aback, Jesus addressed the accompanying crowd: "I've yet to come across this kind of simple trust anywhere in Israel, the very people who are supposed to know about God and how he works."
MSG.10: When the messengers got back home, they found the servant up and well.
MSG.11: Not long after that, Jesus went to the village Nain. His disciples were with him, along with quite a large crowd.
MSG.12: As they approached the village gate, they met a funeral procession—a woman's only son was being carried out for burial. And the mother was a widow.

MSG.14: Then he went over and touched the coffin. The pallbearers stopped. He said, "Young man, I tell you: Get up."
MSG.15: The dead son sat up and began talking. Jesus presented him to his mother.
MSG.16: They all realized they were in a place of holy mystery, that God was at work among them. They were quietly worshipful—and then noisily grateful, calling out among themselves, "God is back, looking to the needs of his people!"

MSG.18: John's disciples reported back to him the news of all these events taking place.
MSG.19: He sent two of them to the Master to ask the question, "Are you the One we've been expecting, or are we still waiting?"

MSG.21: In the next two or three hours Jesus healed many from diseases, distress, and evil spirits. To many of the blind he gave the gift of sight.
MSG.22: Then he gave his answer: "Go back and tell John what you have just seen and heard: The blind see, The lame walk, Lepers are cleansed, The deaf hear, The dead are raised, The wretched of the earth have God's salvation hospitality extended to them.
MSG.23: "Is this what you were expecting? Then count yourselves fortunate!"
MSG.24: After John's messengers left to make their report, Jesus said more about John to the crowd of people. "What did you expect when you went out to see him in the wild? A weekend camper?
MSG.25: Hardly. What then? A sheik in silk pajamas? Not in the wilderness, not by a long shot.
MSG.26: What then? A messenger from God? That's right, a messenger! Probably the greatest messenger you'll ever hear.
MSG.27: He is the messenger Malachi announced when he wrote, I'm sending my messenger on ahead To make the road smooth for you.
MSG.28: "Let me lay it out for you as plainly as I can: No one in history surpasses John the Baptizer, but in the kingdom he prepared you for, the lowliest person is ahead of him.
MSG.29: The ordinary and disreputable people who heard John, by being baptized by him into the kingdom, are the clearest evidence;
MSG.30: the Pharisees and religious officials would have nothing to do with such a baptism, wouldn't think of giving up their place in line to their inferiors.
MSG.31: "How can I account for the people of this generation?
MSG.32: They're like spoiled children complaining to their parents, 'We wanted to skip rope and you were always too tired; we wanted to talk but you were always too busy.'
MSG.33: John the Baptizer came fasting and you called him crazy.
MSG.34: The Son of Man came feasting and you called him a lush.
MSG.35: Opinion polls don't count for much, do they? The proof of the pudding is in the eating."

MSG.37: Just then a woman of the village, the town harlot, having learned that Jesus was a guest in the home of the Pharisee, came with a bottle of very expensive perfume
MSG.38: and stood at his feet, weeping, raining tears on his feet. Letting down her hair, she dried his feet, kissed them, and anointed them with the perfume.

NIV.10: Then the men who had been sent returned to the house and found the servant well.
NIV.11:
Jesus Raises a Widow’s Son
Soon afterward, Jesus went to a town called Nain, and his disciples and a large crowd went along with him.
NIV.12: As he approached the town gate, a dead person was being carried out—the only son of his mother, and she was a widow. And a large crowd from the town was with her.
NIV.13: When the Lord saw her, his heart went out to her and he said, “Don’t cry.”
NIV.14: Then he went up and touched the coffin, and those carrying it stood still. He said, “Young man, I say to you, get up!”
NIV.15: The dead man sat up and began to talk, and Jesus gave him back to his mother.
NIV.16: They were all filled with awe and praised God. “A great prophet has appeared among us,” they said. “God has come to help his people.”
NIV.17: This news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding country.
NIV.18:
Jesus and John the Baptist
John’s disciples told him about all these things. Calling two of them,
NIV.19: he sent them to the Lord to ask, “Are you the one who was to come, or should we expect someone else?”
NIV.20: When the men came to Jesus, they said, “John the Baptist sent us to you to ask, ‘Are you the one who was to come, or should we expect someone else?’”
NIV.21: At that very time Jesus cured many who had diseases, sicknesses and evil spirits, and gave sight to many who were blind.
NIV.22: So he replied to the messengers, “Go back and report to John what you have seen and heard: The blind receive sight, the lame walk, those who have leprosy are cured, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the good news is preached to the poor.
NIV.23: Blessed is the man who does not fall away on account of me.”
NIV.24: After John’s messengers left, Jesus began to speak to the crowd about John: “What did you go out into the desert to see? A reed swayed by the wind?
NIV.25: If not, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? No, those who wear expensive clothes and indulge in luxury are in palaces.
NIV.26: But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
NIV.27:
This is the one about whom it is written:
“‘I will send my messenger ahead of you,
who will prepare your way before you.’
NIV.28: I tell you, among those born of women there is no one greater than John; yet the one who is least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.”
NIV.29: (All the people, even the tax collectors, when they heard Jesus’ words, acknowledged that God’s way was right, because they had been baptized by John.
NIV.30: But the Pharisees and experts in the law rejected God’s purpose for themselves, because they had not been baptized by John.)
NIV.31: “To what, then, can I compare the people of this generation? What are they like?
NIV.32:
They are like children sitting in the marketplace and calling out to each other:
“‘We played the flute for you,
and you did not dance;
we sang a dirge,
and you did not cry.’
NIV.33: For John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, ‘He has a demon.’
NIV.34: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and you say, ‘Here is a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and “sinners.”’
NIV.35: But wisdom is proved right by all her children.”
NIV.36:
Jesus Anointed by a Sinful Woman
Now one of the Pharisees invited Jesus to have dinner with him, so he went to the Pharisee’s house and reclined at the table.
NIV.37: When a woman who had lived a sinful life in that town learned that Jesus was eating at the Pharisee’s house, she brought an alabaster jar of perfume,
NIV.38: and as she stood behind him at his feet weeping, she began to wet his feet with her tears. Then she wiped them with her hair, kissed them and poured perfume on them.
NIV.39: When the Pharisee who had invited him saw this, he said to himself, “If this man were a prophet, he would know who is touching him and what kind of woman she is—that she is a sinner.”
NIV.40:
Jesus answered him, “Simon, I have something to tell you.”
“Tell me, teacher,” he said.
NIV.41: “Two men owed money to a certain moneylender. One owed him five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.
NIV.42: Neither of them had the money to pay him back, so he canceled the debts of both. Now which of them will love him more?”
NIV.43:
Simon replied, “I suppose the one who had the bigger debt canceled.”
“You have judged correctly,” Jesus said.
NIV.44: Then he turned toward the woman and said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? I came into your house. You did not give me any water for my feet, but she wet my feet with her tears and wiped them with her hair.
NIV.45: You did not give me a kiss, but this woman, from the time I entered, has not stopped kissing my feet.
NIV.46: You did not put oil on my head, but she has poured perfume on my feet.
NIV.47: Therefore, I tell you, her many sins have been forgiven—for she loved much. But he who has been forgiven little loves little.”
NIV.48: Then Jesus said to her, “Your sins are forgiven.”
NIV.49: The other guests began to say among themselves, “Who is this who even forgives sins?”
NIV.50: Jesus said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you; go in peace.”
